question: What is a soft fork in Bitcoin?

A split in the Bitcoin blockchain that is not compatible with older versions.

question: What is a 51% attack?

options.label: Where more than half the nodes in the network agree to block a transaction.
When more than 51% of the nodes use the same wallet.
A command in the Bitcoin scripting language that defines an operation to be performed.
A type of attack where someone takes control of more than 51% of the Bitcoin network.

question: Pseudonymous means____?

options.label: Your name is known but other details are not known by the public
All details of transactions done, including wallet address is known but not the identity of owner
Some transaction properties are known while some transaction properties are hidden

question: What is a Bitcoin Merkle root?

options.label: A type of Bitcoin node runners agreement.
A type of code that prevents a 51% attack.
The root hash of all transactions in a block, used to verify the integrity of the data and prevent double-spending.
The root hash of all transactions in a block, used to verify the integrity of miners.

question: What is a Bitcoin nonce?

options.label: A random security password sent to Bitcoin wallets when there is a 51% attack.
A Bitcoin virus that is circulated once two miners verify a block at the same time.
A 32-bit field that miners change as they work on new blocks.
A type of Bitcoin miner that can cause a 51% attack.

question: What is a Bitcoin replay attack?

options.label: A type of Bitcoin transaction that occurs when the price of Bitcoin rises rapidly.
An attack in which a valid transaction is duplicated on a different network.
An attack in which a malicious user manipulates the Bitcoin mining process.
A type of Bitcoin wallet that automatically duplicates transactions.

question: The Bitcoin signature is better and more secure than normal human signatures because____?

options.label: They are signed by a computer and not humans
When you sign it, you can change it in case of theft
They are different for each transaction
They are easy to memorize

question: What is a child-pays-for-parent (CPFP) transaction in the Bitcoin network?

options.label: A transaction where the sender pays a fee for the recipient to receive the Bitcoin.
A transaction where the recipient pays a fee for receiving the Bitcoin.
A transaction where a second transaction increases the fee of the initial transaction in order for both transactions to get prioritized by miners.
A transaction where the sender includes a low fee to save on transaction costs.

question: What is the purpose of a Bitcoin address?

options.label: To mine Bitcoin.
To store private keys.
To receive Bitcoin transactions.
To verify Bitcoin users.

question: What is a hard fork in Bitcoin?

options.label: An agreement by miners to stop verifying Bitcoin transactions.
A split in the Bitcoin blockchain.
A reduction in the block reward given to miners.
A change in the Bitcoin protocol that creates a new blockchain that is not compatible with the old one.

question: What is a Bitcoin transaction fee?

options.label: Bitcoin paid to miners to include a transaction in a block.
A fee charged by a Bitcoin exchange to buy or sell Bitcoin.
A type of Bitcoin peer to peer fee.
A government tax on Bitcoin transactions.

question: When a Bitcoin transaction is marked unconfirmed, it means that

options.label: It has not been broadcasted to the network at all
It has been broadcasted but to just some part of the network
It has been broadcasted to only miners
It has been broadcasted to the network but has not been added to a block

question: What is a Bitcoin deterministic wallet?

options.label: A type of Bitcoin wallet that determines the price of  Bitcoin.
A type of wallet that produces one private key at a time.
A wallet that uses a seed to derive public and private keys.
